questions.prompt: Best way to auto-close an issue when its PR merges?

choices.text: Use GitHub keywords in the PR description: `Closes #1234`, `Fixes #5678`, `Resolves #9012`. When the PR merges to the default branch, GitHub auto-closes the linked issues. Multiple per PR; one issue per line; recognized verbs are close/fix/resolve in any tense. Standard convention; saves the manual close step and keeps issue tracker in sync with code state. Bonus: links appear in both the PR and the issue, providing full audit trail of which code changes closed which issues.

questions.explanation: Use `Closes #1234` in PR description. Auto-closes on merge.

questions.prompt: Issue queue has hundreds of open issues, half from 2+ years ago. Triage strategy?

choices.text: Automate stale handling via the actions/stale action: configure to mark issues stale after N days of inactivity (e.g., 60), then auto-close after another N days (e.g., 7) unless someone comments. The action posts a 'this is stale' comment giving people a chance to re-engage; truly abandoned issues close automatically. Combine with a weekly human triage pass for newly-stale items where you have actual signal on importance. Reduces backlog to actionable work; keeps active issues visible. Standard pattern for open-source maintainers; equally useful for company repos with accumulated cruft.

questions.explanation: actions/stale to auto-mark and auto-close inactive issues + weekly human triage.
